problem.title: Number of Connected Components in an Undirected Graph
problem.description: Given n nodes labeled 0 to n-1 and a list of undirected edges, return the number of connected components in the graph.

example.explanation: Nodes {0,1,2} form one component and nodes {3,4} form another.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Union-Find (Path Compression + Union by Rank) [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Maintain a parent and rank array. For each edge, union the two endpoints; if they already share a root the edge is within an existing component and the count decreases by one. Path compression during find keeps the tree nearly flat, making each operation effectively constant time. This is the most efficient approach with near-linear overall complexity.

complexity.time: O(n · α(n)) where α is the inverse Ackermann function — essentially O(n)
complexity.space: O(n) for parent and rank arrays

example_walkthrough.text: Start with 5 components. Process edge [0,1]: union → 4 components. Process edge [1,2]: union → 3 components. Process edge [3,4]: union → 2 components. Answer: 2.

solutions.title: Solution 2 – BFS/DFS
solutions.description: Build an adjacency list and iterate over all nodes. When an unvisited node is found, run BFS/DFS from it marking all reachable nodes visited, then increment the component count. Every node and edge is visited exactly once, giving O(V+E) time.

complexity.time: O(V + E) — each node and edge is processed once
complexity.space: O(V + E) for the adjacency list and visited array

example_walkthrough.text: Build adjacency list: 0↔1, 1↔2, 3↔4. Iterate: node 0 unvisited → BFS visits 0,1,2, components=1. Node 3 unvisited → BFS visits 3,4, components=2. Nodes 1,2,4 already visited. Return 2.
